glossgroup.title: Glossary
glossgroup.shortdesc: Comprehensive DITA and DITA-OT terminology used throughout the Extense training documentation. Each term includes definitions, abbreviations, usage guidance, and scope notes for chatbot knowledge retrieval.

glossentry.glossdef: A modular, self-contained unit of content in DITA. Topics are the fundamental building blocks and come in typed flavors (concept, task, reference) to match reader intent.

glossBody.glossUsage: Use "topic" when referring to any standalone DITA content unit. Specify the type (concept topic, task topic) when the distinction matters.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Applicable to all DITA versions (1.0+). Topics are the core unit of structured authoring.

glossentry.glossdef: A DITA topic type for explanatory, background, or "what is" content. Concepts answer questions like "What is X?" and "Why does X matter?" They use the <concept> root element and <conbody> for content.

glossBody.glossUsage: Write concepts for introductory, explanatory, or background material. Do not include step-by-step procedures in a concept — use a task topic instead.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: One of the three core DITA information types.

glossentry.glossdef: A DITA topic type for procedural, step-by-step content. Tasks answer "How do I do X?" and use structured <steps> elements with <cmd> and optional <info>, <stepresult>, and <stepxmp> sub-elements.

glossBody.glossUsage: Use task topics for any procedural content. Include <prereq> for prerequisites, <context> for background, and <result> for expected outcomes.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: One of the three core DITA information types. DITA also supports "general task" for less-structured procedures.

glossentry.glossdef: A DITA topic type for lookup-oriented content such as API references, parameter tables, CLI options, or specifications. Uses the <reference> root element with <refbody> containing tables, property lists, or syntax diagrams.

glossBody.glossUsage: Use reference topics for content that users look up rather than read sequentially. Structure with tables and definition lists for scannability.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: One of the three core DITA information types.

glossentry.glossdef: An assembly structure that organizes topics into a hierarchy for publishing. Maps define the table of contents, navigation, and relationship tables. A map does not contain content directly — it references topics.

glossBody.glossUsage: Use maps to assemble topics into deliverables. A single topic can appear in multiple maps. Use bookmaps for book-like outputs with front/back matter.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Maps use the .ditamap file extension. Bookmaps are a specialization of maps.

glossentry.glossdef: A specialization of DITA map that adds book-oriented structures like parts, chapters, appendices, front matter, and back matter. Used for producing book-like deliverables (PDF, printed manuals).

glossBody.glossUsage: Use bookmaps when your output needs book structure (chapters, parts, appendices, TOC, index). Use plain maps for simpler HTML or help outputs.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Available in DITA 1.1+. Requires the bookmap DTD/schema.

glossentry.glossdef: The practice of using different topic structures (concept, task, reference) to match different reader intents and optimize the user experience. This is a core DITA design principle.

glossBody.glossUsage: Apply information typing by asking: "Is this content explaining something (concept), showing how to do something (task), or providing lookup data (reference)?"
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Foundational to the DITA architecture. Custom specializations can add domain-specific types.

glossentry.glossdef: Referencing content instead of duplicating it. DITA provides three primary reuse mechanisms: conref (content reference), keyref (key reference), and conkeyref (key-based content reference). Reuse reduces translation cost, editing effort, and inconsistency.

glossBody.glossUsage: Design content for reuse by keeping topics self-contained, using conref for shared blocks, keyref for variable text, and DITAVAL for variant filtering.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Reuse is often cited as the primary economic justification for adopting DITA.

glossentry.glossdef: Content reference — an attribute that pulls content from another element by matching its @id. The referenced content replaces the referencing element at processing time. This is "copy by reference" at the element level.

glossBody.glossUsage: Use conref to share identical content (warnings, notes, boilerplate paragraphs) across topics. The source and target elements must be the same type.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Available in DITA 1.0+. For key-based content pulling, see conkeyref.

glossentry.glossdef: Key reference — resolves links and variable text via keys defined in the DITA map. Keys provide late binding: the same key can resolve differently in different maps or key scopes, enabling context-dependent content.

glossBody.glossUsage: Use keyrefs for product names, version numbers, and links that may change between deliverables. Define keys in maps using <keydef>.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Available in DITA 1.2+. Key scopes (DITA 1.3) enable multiple definitions of the same key in different contexts.

glossentry.glossdef: Combines keys and content pulling — a key-based content reference that resolves the source element via a key definition, then pulls the content like a conref. Enables flexible multi-context reuse.

glossBody.glossUsage: Use conkeyref when the reuse source varies by context (e.g., different product editions pull from different warehouse topics).
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Available in DITA 1.2+. Requires key definitions in the map.

glossentry.glossdef: Producing content variants by including or excluding elements based on conditional attributes (audience, platform, product, otherprops) and DITAVAL rules. Also called "conditional processing" or "profiling."

glossBody.glossUsage: Apply filtering attributes to elements, then use DITAVAL files to include/exclude/flag content at build time. Plan your filtering strategy before authoring.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: DITA provides four built-in filtering attributes: audience, platform, product, and otherprops.

glossentry.glossdef: An XML file that defines conditional processing rules (include, exclude, flag) applied during DITA-OT builds. DITAVAL files control which content variants appear in the output.

glossBody.glossUsage: Create separate DITAVAL files for each product variant or audience. Reference them in build commands with --filter= or in DITA-OT project files.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: DITAVAL is processed during the filtering stage of DITA-OT preprocessing.

glossentry.glossdef: Extending DITA's type system to represent domain-specific concepts while maintaining compatibility with standard DITA processing. Specializations inherit from base types and can add constraints or new elements.

glossBody.glossUsage: Consider specialization when your domain has recurring structures that don't fit standard topic types. Always specialize from the closest existing base type.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: The "Darwin" in DITA refers to this evolutionary inheritance model.

glossentry.glossdef: Restricting the DITA content model to enforce consistency — for example, removing optional elements or requiring certain metadata. Constraints tighten the model without adding new elements.

glossBody.glossUsage: Use constraints to enforce organization-specific authoring rules (e.g., require shortdesc, prohibit nested topics).
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Available in DITA 1.2+. Constraints are complementary to specialization.

glossentry.glossdef: A named context within a DITA map that limits key definitions to a specific branch. Key scopes enable the same key name to resolve differently in different parts of the same map.

glossBody.glossUsage: Use key scopes when a single deliverable needs different values for the same key. Define with @keyscope on topicref elements.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Available in DITA 1.3+.

glossentry.glossdef: Applying DITAVAL filtering rules to a specific branch (subtree) of a DITA map rather than the entire map. This enables multiple filtered variants within a single build.

glossBody.glossUsage: Use <ditavalref> elements on map branches to apply variant-specific filtering without separate builds.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Available in DITA 1.3+. Processed during the DITA-OT branch-filter preprocessing step.

glossentry.glossdef: A table structure in DITA maps that defines non-hierarchical relationships between topics. Each row links related concepts, tasks, and references, generating "Related links" sections in the output.

glossBody.glossUsage: Use relationship tables instead of inline cross-references when link relationships should be managed centrally in the map.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Relationship tables are processed during DITA-OT preprocessing to generate related-links.

glossentry.glossdef: A special DITA map type that defines controlled vocabularies and taxonomies for metadata values. Subject schemes constrain attribute values to a predefined list, enabling consistent classification.

glossBody.glossUsage: Create subject scheme maps to define enumerated values for filtering attributes. This prevents inconsistent metadata and enables AI-readable taxonomies.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Available in DITA 1.2+. Uses the subjectScheme DTD/schema.

glossentry.glossdef: An open-source publishing engine that transforms DITA XML content into deliverable formats (HTML5, PDF, EPUB, etc.). DITA-OT provides a multi-stage processing pipeline with extension points for customization.

glossBody.glossUsage: DITA-OT is the standard publishing tool for DITA content. Most CCMS products use DITA-OT internally. Current version: 4.3.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Open-source project at github.com/dita-ot/dita-ot. Written in Java with XSLT transforms.

glossentry.glossdef: The output format identifier used by DITA-OT to select a transformation (e.g., html5, pdf, markdown). Each transtype maps to a set of processing modules and XSLT stylesheets.

glossBody.glossUsage: Specify the transtype with -f or --format in DITA-OT commands. Custom plugins can register new transtypes.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Built-in transtypes include html5, pdf, xhtml, eclipsehelp, and markdown.

glossentry.glossdef: The first stage of DITA-OT processing that resolves references, applies filtering, normalizes content, and produces intermediate files. DITA-OT offers two pipelines: legacy "preprocess" and map-first "preprocess2."

glossBody.glossUsage: Use preprocess2 (map-first) for HTML5 output — it is faster and produces cleaner intermediate files.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: preprocess2 is available in DITA-OT 3.0+ and is the default for HTML5 in DITA-OT 4.x.

glossentry.glossdef: An extension package for DITA-OT that can inject XSLT overrides, Ant targets, custom transtypes, processing modules, and static resources. Plugins are the standard way to customize DITA-OT output.

glossBody.glossUsage: Create plugins for any output customization. Use the plugin.xml descriptor to declare dependencies and extension point contributions.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Plugin IDs follow reverse-domain naming (e.g., com.extense.html5.branded).

glossentry.glossdef: A named hook in the DITA-OT processing pipeline where plugins can contribute behavior — XSLT overrides, Ant targets, parameters, or resources.

glossBody.glossUsage: Key extension points: dita.xsl.html5 (HTML5 XSLT), dita.xsl.html5.toc (TOC), dita.conductor.target (Ant targets).
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Extension points are documented in the DITA-OT developer reference.

glossentry.glossdef: Normalized, filtered XML content produced during preprocessing and used by later processing stages. Found in the DITA-OT temp directory.

glossBody.glossUsage: Inspect intermediate files in the temp/ directory to debug preprocessing issues.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Intermediate files include resolved conrefs, applied filtering, and normalized metadata.

glossentry.glossdef: How DITA-OT decides to split or combine topics into output pages or files. Controlled by the @chunk attribute on map elements.

glossBody.glossUsage: Set @chunk="to-content" to combine child topics into one file. Use @chunk="by-topic" to split nested topics.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Chunking behavior changed significantly in DITA 1.3 and DITA-OT 4.x.

glossentry.glossdef: A DITA attribute that passes styling hints to the output. The value is typically rendered as a CSS class name in HTML output or a formatting attribute in PDF.

glossBody.glossUsage: Use outputclass for visual customization that doesn't change content meaning. Example: <codeblock outputclass="language-xml">
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Available on all DITA elements. Processed by XSLT transforms during output generation.

glossentry.glossdef: A DITA-OT project file (.xml) that defines multiple deliverables, contexts, and publications in a single build configuration.

glossBody.glossUsage: Define deliverables in a project file to build multiple outputs from a single command: dita --project=myproject.xml
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Available in DITA-OT 3.4+.

glossentry.glossdef: A content management system designed for component-level (topic-level) content. A CCMS manages DITA topics, maps, images, and metadata as individual components with version control, workflow, and publishing integration.

glossBody.glossUsage: Consider a CCMS when your team needs component-level version control, workflow management, and multi-author collaboration beyond what file-based systems like Git provide.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Extense provides CCMS implementation and integration services.

glossentry.glossdef: Content authored with explicit semantic markup (XML, DITA, etc.) that separates content from presentation, enables reuse, and supports automated processing.

glossBody.glossUsage: Structured content is the foundation of scalable documentation programs. DITA is the most widely adopted structured content standard for technical documentation.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Extense specializes in structured content strategy and migration from unstructured to structured formats.

glossentry.glossdef: The process of converting existing documentation from unstructured formats (Word, FrameMaker, HTML, flat XML) into structured DITA XML. Includes analysis, information architecture design, conversion, and validation.

glossBody.glossUsage: Plan content migration by first analyzing existing content, designing the DITA information architecture, then converting with a combination of automated tools and manual review.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: Extense offers end-to-end content migration services.

glossentry.glossdef: Content that is structurally rich, semantically categorized, and machine-processable — enabling automated discovery, personalization, and AI-driven delivery.

glossBody.glossUsage: Transform standard DITA content into intelligent content by adding rich metadata, controlled vocabularies, content graphs, and machine-readable annotations.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: This training guide demonstrates intelligent content principles through its subject scheme, enriched prologs, and chatbot-ready metadata.

glossentry.glossdef: An AI architecture that enhances language model responses by retrieving relevant content from a knowledge base before generating answers. RAG combines vector search over document chunks with LLM generation for grounded, accurate responses.

glossBody.glossUsage: Structure DITA content for RAG by ensuring each topic has clear metadata, a good shortdesc, and proper chunking boundaries at section level.
glossBody.glossScopeNote: The Extense chatbot uses RAG over DITA-derived content chunks.
